scope: Pass B full-surface review of a small, structurally unusual API: legacy exposes exactly one caller-configured dataset query endpoint (query_path/query_params are entirely operator-supplied at connection-config time, not per-stream) -- this remains the only real per-dataset data query surface and is unchanged from the wave1 Tier-2 StreamHook migration. This revision adds ONE genuinely new, declaratively-shapeable resource researched directly against the live API: the Bureau's own public dataset discovery catalog (GET /data.json, confirmed live via a direct fetch during this review -- a DCAT-formatted JSON object listing every published dataset with its own variables/geography/tags/groups sub-resource links), which is a fixed, uniform-shaped resource unlike the caller-driven query endpoint. Per-dataset sub-resources (variables.json, geography.json, tags.json, groups.json, examples.json -- one set per one of thousands of published datasets, each dataset's own path embedded in the catalog response) are excluded: there is no fixed, finite endpoint list to enumerate (the set of valid paths is itself the catalog's own row count, thousands of entries, each a distinct URL), matching the exact reasoning that scoped the original query stream.

excluded.reason: per-dataset variable-metadata sub-resource; one distinct URL per published dataset (thousands, enumerated only by reading the datasets stream's own c_variablesLink field per row) -- not a fixed, finite endpoint this manifest can enumerate directly, matching the reasoning that scoped the caller-driven query endpoint itself
per-dataset geography-hierarchy sub-resource; same thousands-of-distinct-URLs reasoning as the variables.json sub-resource
per-dataset tag-metadata sub-resource; same thousands-of-distinct-URLs reasoning as the variables.json sub-resource
per-dataset variable-group sub-resource; same thousands-of-distinct-URLs reasoning as the variables.json sub-resource

per-dataset example-query documentation sub-resource (illustrative curl examples, not data); same thousands-of-distinct-URLs reasoning as the variables.json sub-resource, and non-data even where reachable
